---
package: "openpipeline"
version: "v4.2.0"
title: "velocyto"
namespace: "Velocity"
description: "Runs the velocity analysis on a BAM file, outputting a loom file."
type: "module"
toc: false
latest: "true"
subtitle: "openpipeline v4.2.0"
---

::: {.column-margin}

### Info

ID: `velocyto`  
Namespace: `velocity`

### Links

[{{< fa brands github >}} View source code](https://github.com/openpipelines-bio/openpipeline/tree/v4.2.0/src/velocity/velocyto)

[![](/images/viash-hub-logo.svg){.vh-icon} Browse all versions](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline)

[![](/images/viash-hub-logo.svg){.vh-icon} Get run instructions](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline/v4.2.0/components/velocity/velocyto)

:::




## Argument group

### Arguments

|Name              |Description                                                                                                                                                                                |Attributes                                       |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--input`         |Path to BAM file                                                                                                                                                                           |`file`, required, example: `"input.bam"`         |
|`--transcriptome` |Path to GTF file                                                                                                                                                                           |`file`, required, example: `"transcriptome.gtf"` |
|`--barcode`       |Valid barcodes file, to filter the bam. If --bcfile is not specified all the cell barcodes will be included. Cell barcodes should be specified in the bcfile as the 'CB' tag for each read |`file`, example: `"barcodes.txt"`                |
|`--without_umi`   |foo                                                                                                                                                                                        |`boolean_true`                                   |
|`--output`        |Velocyto loom file                                                                                                                                                                         |`file`, required, example: `"output.loom"`       |
|`--logic`         |The logic to use for the filtering.                                                                                                                                                        |`string`, default: `"Default"`                   |

## Authors


  * Robrecht Cannoodt [{{< fa brands github >}}](https://github.com/rcannood) [{{< fa brands orcid >}}](https://orcid.org/0000-0003-3641-729X) [{{< fa brands linkedin >}}](https://linkedin.com/in/robrechtcannoodt) (maintainer)

## Example commands

You can run the pipeline using `nextflow run`.

### View help

You can use `--help` as a parameter to get an overview of the possible parameters.

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline \
  -r v4.2.0 -latest \
  -main-script target/nextflow/velocity/velocyto/main.nf \
  --help
```

### Run command

<details>
<summary>Example of `params.yaml`</summary>

Generate a `params.yaml` for this component on [Viash Hub](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline/v4.2.0/components/velocity/velocyto).

</details>

Run locally with:

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline \
  -r v4.2.0 -latest \
  -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/velocity/velocyto/main.nf \
  -params-file params.yaml
```

:::{.callout-note}
Replace `-profile docker` with `-profile podman` or `-profile singularity` depending on the desired backend.
:::

Running on Seqera or through Viash Hub is explained in the [guides](/guides/index.qmd).
